expectations.text: Moves incomplete issues to Sprint 6 via POST /rest/agile/1.0/sprint/{sprint6Id}/issue with an issues array
expectations.evidence: Response uses POST /issue/{ISSUE_KEY}/move (line 78), POST /issue/bulk (line 91), and PUT /issue/{ISSUE_KEY} (line 291). None use the correct agile API endpoint POST /rest/agile/1.0/sprint/{sprintId}/issue with issues array.

expectations.text: Closes the sprint via PUT /rest/agile/1.0/sprint/{sprintId} with state: 'closed'
expectations.evidence: Response uses POST /sprint/{SPRINT_5_ID}/close (lines 111, 301) instead of PUT /rest/agile/1.0/sprint/{sprintId} with state: 'closed' payload.

expectations.text: Marks the JIRA version released via PUT /rest/api/3/version/{versionId} with released: true and releaseDate
expectations.evidence: Response correctly uses PUT /version/{VERSION_ID} with released: true and releaseDate (lines 151-158, 307-310).

expectations.text: If fixVersions is mentioned, uses correct array-of-objects format: [{"name": "v1.5.0"}]. If not mentioned, mark as passed.
expectations.evidence: fixVersions is not mentioned in the response.

expectations.text: Creates GitHub release using 'gh release create v1.5.0 --title ... --notes ... --target main' (or --generate-notes)
expectations.evidence: Response uses 'gh release create v1.5.0 --title "Version 1.5.0" --notes "..." --target main' (lines 177-200, 315-320).

expectations.text: Steps in correct order: find incomplete issues, move to Sprint 6, close Sprint 5, release JIRA version, publish GitHub release
expectations.evidence: Complete automation script follows correct sequence: Task 1 finds/moves issues (lines 269-297), Task 2 closes sprint (lines 300-302), Task 3 releases version (lines 305-311), Task 4 creates GitHub release (lines 314-320).

expectations.text: Uses JQL to find incomplete issues in Sprint 5, e.g., project = ENG AND sprint = 'Sprint 5' AND status != Done
expectations.evidence: Response uses JQL query 'project = ENG AND sprint = {SPRINT_5_ID} AND status NOT IN (Done, Closed, Resolved)' (lines 62, 280). Uses sprint ID variable and status NOT IN which is valid equivalent.
